cli.info.heading: Supported formats
cli.info.action.pack_reason: best when you need an immediate output path, size, volume count, and verify-next cue
cli.info.action.unpack_reason: best when overwrite, symlink, smart-folder, and safety limits must stay visible
cli.info.action.inspect_form: support map + format coverage + runtime inventory
cli.info.action.inspect_reason: best when choosing a format or checking whether a bridge runtime is available
cli.info.action.live_form: operation cockpit + signal matrix + transfer matrix + action queue
cli.info.action.live_reason: best for compression/extraction where phase, ETA, signal matrix, elapsed time, current object, action queue, and next step matter
cli.info.action.automation_form: classic rows or JSON without ANSI/redraw
cli.info.action.automation_reason: best for shell scripts, CI, and predictable parsing
cli.info.palette.best_default: default brand look and app icon continuity
cli.info.palette.best_brand: short brand option for the approved app icon colors
cli.info.palette.best_icon: explicitly use the selected app icon palette
cli.info.palette.best_cascade: brighter modern tables while preserving the approved app icon teal
cli.info.palette.best_daylight: bright teal/sky output for modern tables and live progress without dark chrome
cli.info.palette.best_foam: soft app-icon colors with a bright ice-blue highlight and no dark chrome
cli.info.palette.best_skyline: brighter blue-led terminal output while keeping the approved icon colors
cli.info.palette.best_aero: lighter sky-led modern output while staying in the approved icon palette
cli.info.palette.best_crest: brighter teal/sky command demos without dark chrome
cli.info.palette.best_halo: bright teal/sky output for richer modern progress and tables
cli.info.palette.best_kinetic: approved teal with a more energetic sky accent for live transfer matrices
cli.info.palette.best_radiant: approved teal with a brighter sky-glass highlight for modern progress dashboards
cli.info.palette.best_surge: approved teal with vivid sky-blue accents for live HUDs and dense tables
cli.info.palette.best_fancy: more luminous modern terminals without dark chrome
cli.info.palette.best_vivid: more vivid modern output without dark chrome
cli.info.palette.best_plain: terminals that cannot render truecolor well
cli.info.cheatsheet.create: Create ZIP, 7z, TAR, WIM, or SQZ output.
cli.info.cheatsheet.extract: Unpack with overwrite and safety controls.
cli.info.cheatsheet.update: Add, delete, rename, or move entries atomically.
cli.info.cheatsheet.recover_use: Use SQZ or PAR2 recovery flows for repairable archives.
cli.info.legend: Legend: C=create X=extract T=test U=update S=split E=encrypt N=hide names; ✓ supported, · unavailable.
cli.info.note.built_in_archives: Ready now; no external tools required.
cli.info.note.external_archives: Runtime shows this machine's external tools; --json exposes exact paths.

cli.info.col.matrix: C X T U S E N

gui.app.name: Squallz
gui.archive.return_current_detail: {archive} remains open; return to its file list without losing this tool setup.
gui.external_task.waiting_body: The selected action will open here.
gui.external_task.no_selection: No file was provided for this action
gui.external_task.requires_desktop_servi: This action requires the desktop service
gui.archive.info_subtitle: Current archive, selection, extraction target, encoding, and volume state.
gui.empty.no_archive_summary: Open an archive to browse entries, inspect metadata, and run archive actions.
gui.update.target_review: Review rename and move targets first
gui.preview.select_file: Select one file and choose Preview.
gui.preview.open_archive_first: Open an archive before previewing entries
gui.preview.select_one: Select one entry to preview or open
gui.preview.select_archive: Select an archive-like entry, such as .zip, .7z, .dmg or .7z.001
gui.preview.nested_failed: Nested archive preview failed or unsupported
gui.preview.reveal_failed: Cannot reveal the preview file in the file manager
gui.preview.open_failed: Cannot open the preview file with the system preview app
gui.recovery.requires_recovery_data: Verify can detect corruption, but repair requires PAR2 or SQZ recovery data created earlier.
gui.recovery.no_overpromise_body: When damage exceeds recovery capacity, extract readable files or export a report; full repair needs more recovery blocks.
gui.selection.actions_hint: Extract, preview, or copy the selected files without leaving the archive.
gui.extract.smart_folder_hint: Smart extract creates a containing folder when roots are mixed, preventing files from scattering into Downloads.
gui.batch.ready_continue_hint: Ready archives can continue without global failure.
gui.history.title: Operation history
gui.recent.title: Recent Archives
gui.recent.subtitle: Reopen local archives you used recently. Squallz keeps only the file paths, never archive contents.
gui.recent.open_to_start: Open an archive to start this list.
gui.recent.privacy_body: Recent files are stored locally as paths for quick reopening. Passwords and archive contents are not stored here.
gui.quick.title: Quick actions
gui.quick.subtitle: Jump without changing layout
gui.settings.section.colors.detail: Theme color presets and custom accent
gui.settings.section.password_book.detai: Secret-store status and saved archive secrets
gui.appearance.title: Interface and display
gui.appearance.subtitle: Mode switching is primary. Theme Colors sits under Appearance as a focused second-level page.
gui.appearance.switch_context_body: Open archive, selection, task state, theme, and theme colors remain shared.
gui.appearance.current_mode_body: Mode switching changes shell and density only; archive state and jobs stay intact.
gui.appearance.colors_body: Theme color presets and custom accent controls have their own page.
gui.appearance.mode_note_title: Mode switching keeps the current archive and task state.
gui.appearance.mode_note_body: Only shell layout and density tokens change; business state remains shared.
gui.appearance.pages_note_body: Theme color presets are still part of Appearance, but they live in the Theme Colors subpage so this panel stays compact.
gui.classic.appearance.title: Options · Appearance
gui.colors.title: Theme colors and custom accent
gui.colors.subtitle: Recommended theme color presets and custom accent controls live in this Appearance subpage instead of crowding Interface mode.
gui.classic.colors.title: Options · Appearance · Theme Colors
gui.classic.empty_workbench_hint: Archive editing controls appear after an archive is open.
gui.empty.title: Drop an archive to browse, or files and folders to compress
gui.list.col.name: Name
gui.list.zipcrypto_tip: Uses legacy ZipCrypto encryption, which is weak
gui.list.garbled_tip: File name may use a different encoding
gui.banner.garbled.fail: Could not detect the encoding. Please choose one manually.
gui.banner.garbled.suggest: File names may be encoded as {encoding}
gui.banner.zipcrypto: This archive uses legacy ZipCrypto encryption, which is weak
gui.compress.title: Compress
gui.compress.level.unsupported: This format does not support compression levels
gui.compress.encrypt_names.zip_tip: ZIP cannot encrypt file names. Use 7z to hide the file list.
gui.compress.split.invalid: Volume size must be at least 1 MB
gui.compress.target_exists: A file with this name exists; you will be asked to overwrite
gui.compress_source.title: Choose What to Compress
gui.compress_source.body: Pick files or a folder first. Both choices use the same compression options next.
gui.compress_source.files_desc: Select one or more files from Finder.
gui.compress_source.folder_desc: Select a folder and keep its contents together.
gui.extract.title: Extract {name}
gui.extract.smart_tip: Extracts directly when the archive has a single root folder; otherwise wraps loose files in a folder named after the archive
gui.extract.safe_subtitle: Destination preview, smart folder behavior, conflicts, passwords, and safety limits are visible before the job starts.
gui.extract.snapshot_hint: Smart extract captures the current safety settings when the job starts.
gui.extract.blocked_conditions_body: Path traversal, case collision, reserved Windows names, and symlink escapes stop the job before writing.
gui.extract.classic_subtitle: Choose the final folder, preview smart extract behavior, and review conflicts before writing files.
gui.extract.safety_blocked: Zip Slip, bomb ratio, reserved names, symlink escape blocked
gui.extract.smart_deterministic_body: Mixed archive roots get a containing folder; a single root folder extracts directly.
gui.conflict.title: File Already Exists
gui.conflict.body: The destination already contains a file with this name:
gui.conflict.case_note: This file system is case-insensitive; {a} and {b} are treated as the same name
gui.conflict.apply_all: Do the same for the remaining {count} conflicts
gui.conflict.policy_body: Decisions can apply per file or to the remaining conflict set; default is never silent overwrite.
gui.queue.title: Tasks
gui.task.control_detail: The current control request is waiting for archive engine acknowledgement.
gui.task.control_cancel_detail: Stopping at the next safe checkpoint. New archive actions stay blocked until the engine confirms cancellation.
gui.task.control_pause_detail: Pausing at the next safe checkpoint. Progress stays visible while the engine finishes the current chunk.
gui.task.control_resume_detail: Waiting for the engine to report running again.
gui.task.result_ready: Task finished; result details are available in the related tool
gui.task.result_failed: The task failed before producing a result
gui.task.one_at_a_time_notice: Finish or cancel the current task before starting another one
gui.task.starting_notice: Task is starting. Wait for the progress window before starting another operation
gui.queue.controls_require_running_job: Task controls require a running task
gui.table.name: Name
gui.queue.target_busy: Target file is in use by another task
gui.convert.title: Convert Archive
gui.convert.subtitle: Convert the current archive to the opposite launch format without extracting files into a temporary folder.
gui.convert.auto_target_hint: ZIP sources convert to 7Z; other sources convert to ZIP.
gui.convert.contract_body: The task runs through the same archive engine path as the CLI convert command; source archives are not modified.
gui.convert.ready_body: Destination is derived next to the source archive. Review it before starting.
gui.convert.guard_body: Passwords, encoding overrides, and archive safety checks stay explicit; conversion never implies repair data.
gui.convert.same_format_note: Target format equals the source; it will be recompressed
gui.convert.same_path: Target cannot be the same as the source
gui.convert.streaming_note: Conversion is streamed; no temporary extraction
gui.settings.title: Settings
gui.settings.performance.threads_hint: Worker threads used by compression engines that support it.
gui.settings.performance.scope: Applies to newly submitted compression and conversion jobs. Current build wires this to Zstandard-based outputs; other formats keep their automatic behavior until their engines expose real controls.
gui.settings.limits.output_hint: Upper bound on total data written by a single extraction
gui.settings.limits.entries_hint: Upper bound on the number of entries in one archive
gui.settings.limits.ratio_hint: Suspiciously high ratios are blocked to prevent decompression bombs
gui.settings.password_book.unavailable: Persistent password storage is not available in this environment.
gui.settings.password_book.no_archive: Open an encrypted archive to manage its saved password.
gui.settings.password_book.saved: The current archive password is saved in Keychain.
gui.settings.password_book.not_saved: The current archive has no saved password.
gui.first_run.title: Choose an interface mode
gui.first_run.body: Pick the interface that matches your habits. You can switch later in Settings.
gui.first_run.modern_desc: A restrained macOS / Keka / Finder style for quick everyday compression and extraction.
gui.first_run.classic_desc: A denser WinRAR / 7-Zip style for users who prefer traditional archive managers.
gui.password.title: Password Required
gui.password.hint: “{name}” is encrypted. The password is remembered for this session.
gui.password.saved_boundary_body: Saved passwords stay behind the system secret-store boundary; task status and settings only show status.
gui.security.zipslip.title: Unsafe Archive Blocked
gui.security.zipslip.what: The archive tried to write files outside the destination folder.
gui.security.zipslip.why: This is a common malicious-archive technique (Zip Slip). Extraction was stopped.
gui.security.symlink.title: Symlink Escape Blocked
gui.security.symlink.what: The archive tried to bypass the destination folder through a symbolic link.
gui.security.symlink.why: Later files could be written anywhere the link points to. Extraction was stopped.
gui.security.limit.title: Safety Limit Exceeded
gui.security.limit.what: The extraction exceeded a safety limit ({limit}).
gui.security.limit.why: Extraction stops at the threshold to prevent decompression bombs from filling your disk or memory. If you trust this file, raise the limit and retry.
gui.error.io.title: Read/Write Error
gui.error.unsupported.title: Cannot Handle This File Type
gui.error.corrupt.title: Archive Is Damaged
gui.error.corrupt.hint: The file may be incomplete. Try downloading it again.
gui.error.corrupt.volume_missing: Volume {name} is missing. Keep all volumes in the same folder.
gui.error.disk_full.title: Not Enough Disk Space
gui.error.disk_full.body: {required} needed, {available} available. Free up space and retry.
gui.error.dependency.title: Additional Component Required
gui.error.dependency.body: Handling this format requires {name}. Install it and retry.
gui.error.other.title: Operation Failed
gui.toast.unsafe_names: {count} file names contained unsafe characters and were adjusted
gui.toast.file_busy: File is in use by another task
gui.notify.done.title: Task Finished
gui.notify.failed.title: Task Failed
gui.batch.title: Batch Extract
gui.batch.body: Extract {count} archives each into its own folder?
gui.batch.review_subtitle: Every target folder is previewed before work starts. Password or volume issues block only the affected archive.
gui.batch.classic_subtitle: Review every archive, target folder, password state, and blocked item before tasks start.
gui.batch.continue_ready_hold_blocked: Continue ready archives, hold blocked archive
gui.batch.status_ready_rule: Ready archives can continue; blocked archive waits
gui.quit.title: Tasks Still Running
gui.quit.body: {count} tasks are not finished. Quitting will cancel them.
gui.settings.folder.picker_requires_desk: Folder picker requires the desktop service
gui.classic.settings.general.title: Options · General
gui.settings.general.title: General app behavior
gui.settings.general.subtitle: Startup, language, default folders, update prompts, and file-open policy stay separate from Appearance.
gui.settings.general.classic_subtitle: Startup, language, defaults, and update prompts are explicit app behavior settings.
gui.settings.general.reveal_after_extrac: Reveal extracted destination in {fileManager} after successful extract
gui.settings.general.classic_reveal_afte: Classic reveal extracted destination in {fileManager} after successful extract
gui.settings.general.open_with_value_app: Candidate only · never steal default apps
gui.settings.general.boundary_body: Password, recovery, unsafe path, and conflict prompts remain visible in their workflows.
gui.settings.general.saved_detail: Language: {language} · Default folder: {folder} · Reveal after extract {reveal}
gui.settings.general.saved_preview: General settings saved for this preview · locale applied locally
gui.classic.settings.security.title: Options · Security
gui.settings.security.title: Extraction safety and privacy
gui.settings.security.subtitle: Dangerous archive behavior is blocked by defaults; advanced overrides require explicit review.
gui.settings.security.classic_subtitle: Safety limits are captured when a job starts; dangerous archive writes are blocked before disk changes.
gui.settings.security.disabled_until_con: Available after every affected task is connected
Stream buffer memory is configured in Performance; per-format sandbox switches stay hidden until every affected task uses the setting snapshot.
gui.classic.settings.performance.title: Options · Performance
gui.settings.performance.title: Performance and scale behavior
gui.settings.performance.subtitle: Only controls already supported by archive engine settings are active; speculative resource switches stay disabled.
gui.settings.performance.classic_subtitl: Only supported performance settings are active in the UI.
gui.settings.performance.worker_threads_: Manual thread count is available for supported formats; others keep format defaults.
gui.settings.performance.stream_buffer_b: Caps Squallz-owned copy buffers; format encoders may keep their own dictionaries.
gui.classic.settings.password_book.title: Options · Password Book
gui.settings.password_book.title: Saved archive passwords
gui.settings.password_book.subtitle: Squallz stores saved archive passwords only through the system secret store boundary.
gui.settings.password_book.classic_subti: Saved archive passwords are controlled through the system secret-store boundary.
gui.settings.password_book.secret_store_: {platform} {secretStore} when available; other platforms stay behind their own secret stores
gui.settings.password_book.no_plaintext_: No localStorage, no settings.json, no logs
Password Book UI never displays or exports saved password material.
gui.settings.password_book.non_macos_pen: Use each platform's system secret store
gui.settings.password_book.open_archive_: Open an archive to check saved password status
gui.settings.password_book.current_has_s: Current archive has a saved secret-store entry
gui.settings.password_book.prompt_or_sav: Prompt or save after unlocking this archive
gui.settings.password_book.open_archive_: Open an archive before checking Password Book status
gui.settings.password_book.status_unavai: Password Book status unavailable in this preview
gui.settings.password_book.no_open_passw: No open archive password to forget
gui.settings.password_book.could_not_for: Could not forget current archive password
gui.settings.password_book.status_only_b: Saved passwords never cross the frontend boundary as plaintext; UI shows status only.
gui.password.book_can_unlock: Password Book can unlock if needed
gui.password.ask_only_if_required: Ask only if the archive requires it
gui.password.session_only_book_flow: Session only · Password Book saves through unlock flow
gui.password.return_to_prompt: Return to prompt, do not fail whole batch
gui.password.secret_store_supplies_direc: It only shows available/saved status; the system secret store supplies passwords directly to archive operations.
gui.password.session_zeroize: Session cache: memory cleared after use.
gui.classic.settings.integration.title: Options · File Associations
gui.settings.integration.title: File associations and context menus
gui.settings.integration.subtitle: {platform} starts as an {openWith} candidate. Other platforms use their own association and file-manager action matrices.
gui.settings.integration.classic_subtitl: File association and context menu controls are explicit, platform-specific, and reversible.
gui.settings.integration.macos_note: File-manager services and quick actions are designed separately from document type registration.
gui.settings.integration.platform_note: {fileManager} services and quick actions are designed separately from document type registration.
gui.settings.integration.platform_rule_b: {platform} uses {openWith} and {fileManager} actions; other platforms use their own association and file-manager actions.
gui.settings.integration.action_wim: Browse, extract via 7zz/7z; create via wimlib
gui.settings.integration.finder_actions_: {fileManager} actions not installed in this session
gui.settings.integration.platform_action: {fileManager} actions not installed in this session
gui.settings.integration.install_detail: Installs Checksum, Extract Here, Extract to Folder, Compress to 7Z, and Test Archive.
gui.settings.integration.none_installed_: No integrations installed on this platform
gui.settings.integration.not_installed_y: This platform integration is not installed yet
gui.settings.integration.status_requires: {fileManager} integration status requires the desktop service
gui.settings.integration.removal_require: {fileManager} integration removal requires the desktop service
gui.settings.integration.dev_build_polic: Squallz does not take over default apps automatically. File-manager actions are explicit opt-in integrations.
gui.settings.integration.separate_rule_t: Associations and context menus are separate
gui.settings.integration.separate_rule_b: They are shown together for review, but installed through platform-specific mechanisms.
gui.integration.finder.cli_not_found.mes: Install sqz or set SQUALLZ_CLI to enable Finder Quick Actions.
gui.integration.file_manager.cli_not_fou: Install sqz or set SQUALLZ_CLI to enable file-manager actions.
gui.integration.explorer.cli_not_found.m: Install sqz or set SQUALLZ_CLI to enable File Explorer actions.
gui.format.note.rar_read_only: Read-only; no RAR creation or .rev claim
gui.format.note.wim_external: WIM create requires wimlib-imagex; read uses 7zz/7z
gui.format.note.compound_tar: Compound TAR stream; no encryption claim
gui.create.password_support_body: Password support follows the selected archive format capability.
gui.create.recovery_explicit_body: Recovery jobs remain explicit so the app never implies uncreated repair data.
gui.format.coverage.title: Format coverage
gui.format.coverage.create_limited: {source} · create controls stay limited to release-claimed writable formats.
gui.settings.inspector.storage_body: Startup, language, default folders, and update behavior belong in settings storage, not archive job specs.
gui.settings.security.snapshot_body: Extraction jobs capture safety limits when submitted so later setting changes do not mutate running work.
gui.settings.performance.zstandard_worke: Only Zstandard currently honors manual workers; unsupported format controls stay hidden until their engines use the setting snapshot.
gui.checksum.subtitle: Calculate local file digests or verify a manifest with the same engine exposed by sqz checksum.
gui.duplicates.subtitle: Scan local folders with the same BLAKE3 duplicate detector exposed by sqz duplicates; no cleanup action is run.
gui.duplicates.min_size_invalid: Use a whole number of bytes, 0 or more
gui.password.prompt_boundary_body: Unlock only the archive that requested credentials. No password is written to logs, settings, or task status.
gui.password.no_plaintext_storage: No plaintext password stored in settings or task status
gui.conflict.dialog_boundary_body: The decision never silently escapes this dialog; batch jobs preserve per-archive conflict state.
gui.recovery.title: Recovery
gui.recovery.full_repair_blocked_body: Full repair is blocked because damaged or missing blocks exceed available recovery data.
gui.recovery.no_failed_result_loaded: No failed recovery result is loaded.
gui.recovery.full_repair_blocked_safe: Full repair blocked. No destructive write will start.
gui.recovery.no_failure_result_loaded: No recovery failure result is loaded.
gui.recovery.readable_entries_partial: Readable entries can be listed and extracted to a separate folder.
gui.recovery.open_and_verify_first: Open an archive and run recovery verification first.
gui.recovery.block_math_visible_body: Block math is visible so repair promises match the actual Reed-Solomon capacity.
gui.recovery.dual_separate_jobs: Run SQZ and PAR2 as separate jobs
gui.recovery.rerun_before_success: Repair or extract re-runs verification before reporting success.
gui.recovery.full_blocked_partial_only: Full repair blocked · partial extract only
gui.recovery.route_from_create_current_a: Recovery opened for the current archive
gui.recovery.route_from_create_open_arch: Recovery opens separate Protect, Verify, Repair, and Export jobs. Open an archive first.
gui.colors.contrast_guard_body: Error, warning, success, and recovery state colors stay semantic; custom accent only changes brand chrome and selection.
gui.colors.aa_contrast_guard_body: Generated hover, focus, and selected colors are clamped before the Apply button becomes available.
gui.colors.palette.aqua.name: Aqua Graphite
gui.colors.palette.aqua.note: Clean teal, graphite neutrals, calm utility feel.
gui.colors.palette.sage.name: Sage Titanium
gui.colors.palette.sage.note: Muted sage with titanium gray for restrained desktop work.
gui.colors.palette.nordic.name: Nordic Blue
gui.colors.palette.nordic.note: Cool professional blue without turning the app into one flat hue.
gui.colors.palette.copper.name: Ink Copper
gui.colors.palette.copper.note: Ink neutrals with a restrained copper accent for classic tools.
gui.colors.palette.aubergine.name: Aubergine Steel
gui.colors.palette.aubergine.note: Deep wine-plum balanced by steel so it avoids purple overload.
gui.colors.palette.mono.name: Monochrome Pro
gui.colors.palette.mono.note: Graphite-first UI for users who want the app to disappear.
gui.colors.palette.custom.name: Custom Accent
gui.colors.palette.custom.note: Saved custom color with automatic light and dark contrast.
gui.colors.light_dark_auto: Light and dark variants are generated automatically
gui.colors.direct_accent: Direct accent preview · semantic colors stay locked
gui.settings.general.status_rule: Safety and password warnings remain visible
gui.appearance.status_colors: Theme Colors available as Appearance subpage
gui.colors.status_contrast: Contrast guard active · semantic colors locked
gui.settings.security.status_never_disab: Zip Slip and symlink escape never disabled
gui.settings.performance.snapshot_policy: Settings are captured when each job starts.
gui.settings.performance.status_snapshot: Resource snapshot captured when jobs start
gui.settings.password_book.status_bounda: No plaintext password leaves secret-store boundary
gui.settings.snapshot.auto_resources_pre: Auto resources preview · desktop service unavailable
gui.settings.number.invalid_range: {label} must be a whole number from {min} to {max}
gui.settings.security.saved_preview: Security settings saved for this preview · desktop service unavailable
gui.settings.security.reset_preview: Security settings reset for this preview · desktop service unavailable
gui.settings.performance.saved_preview: Performance settings saved for this preview · desktop service unavailable
gui.settings.performance.reset_auto_reso: Performance settings reset to automatic resources
gui.settings.performance.reset_preview: Performance settings reset for this preview · desktop service unavailable
gui.colors.saved_preview: Theme colors saved for this preview · desktop service unavailable
gui.appearance.saved_preview: Appearance settings saved for this preview · desktop service unavailable
gui.mode.saved_preview_desktop_unavailab: Interface mode saved for this preview · desktop service unavailable
gui.theme.saved_preview_desktop_unavaila: Theme saved for this preview · desktop service unavailable
gui.density.saved_preview_desktop_unavai: Density saved for this preview · desktop service unavailable
gui.drop.active: Drop archives to open, or files and folders to create an archive
gui.create.measuring_source_bytes: Measuring source bytes and exclude rules
gui.create.scanning_after_excludes: Scanning local inputs after exclude rules · {count} entries found so far.
gui.create.measure_real_bytes_body: Squallz will measure real input bytes after the source picker; it will not guess compressed output size.
gui.create.final_volume_count_after_writ: Final volume count appears after the archive is written.
gui.create.volume_output_budget_guide: {split} · output-budget guide {count} volumes; final count depends on compression.
gui.create.disk_preflight_body_pending: After choosing a destination, Squallz checks the target volume before the task starts.
gui.create.disk_preflight_body: {available} available in {path}; {required} reserved as a conservative output budget.
gui.create.temp_preflight_body_pending: Squallz also checks the system temporary folder before the create task starts.
gui.create.temp_preflight_body: {available} available in {path}; {required} reserved for temporary rewrite headroom.

gui.first_run.modern_body: Calm macOS utility, guided archive tasks, inspector-first safety.
gui.first_run.classic_body: Dense command bar, detailed archive table, keyboard-friendly operations.
gui.first_run.mode_changed_settings: Mode can be changed in Settings
gui.create.real_preflight_body: Edit rules, then choose files or folders; Squallz measures real input bytes before the compression task starts.
gui.create.rar_not_launch_claim: RAR creation is not a launch claim
gui.create.keep_one_custom_profile: Keep at least one custom profile
gui.create.custom_profile_limit: Maximum 8 custom profiles; delete one to save another
gui.create.custom_level_invalid: Use a compression level from 1 to 9
gui.excludes.title: Excludes
gui.excludes.create_hint: One glob, folder, or extension per line.
gui.excludes.folder_scan_hint: Applied only when the target is a folder.
gui.excludes.duplicate_hint: Skip noisy folders before duplicate hashing.
gui.create.recovery_separate_jobs: Creating the archive and generating recovery data are separate jobs; use Recovery when you want PAR2 or SQZ repair evidence.
gui.checksum.title: Verify files without changing them
gui.checksum.modern_subtitle: Compute SHA-256, SHA-512, SHA-384, SHA-224, SHA-1, MD5, BLAKE3, or CRC32 with the shared core engine, or verify a checksum manifest.
gui.checksum.cli_contract_body: This page maps to sqz checksum and sqz checksum --check; JSON result fields stay aligned with automation output.
gui.duplicates.route_from_checksum: Find identical local files with BLAKE3
gui.recovery.route_from_checksum: Test, protect, repair, and export archives
gui.duplicates.title: Find duplicate local files
gui.duplicates.modern_subtitle: BLAKE3 hashes are computed by the shared core engine; this scan never deletes, moves, links, or modifies files.
gui.duplicates.smaller_ignored: Smaller files are ignored before hashing
gui.duplicates.potential_space: Potential space if one copy per group remains
gui.duplicates.non_destructive_body: The scan never cleans up, hard-links, deletes, moves, or modifies files automatically.
gui.duplicates.route_to_create: Use the same exclude semantics before compression
gui.duplicates.route_to_batch: Review targets before starting archive work
gui.password.session_only_separate_book: Session only for this job; saved passwords use the separate Password Book flow
gui.password.manual_wins_body: Manual password wins over saved password. Failed saved passwords fall back to this prompt.
gui.recovery.cannot_fully_repair: This archive cannot be fully repaired
gui.recovery.explicit_capacity_body: Squallz must be explicit: available recovery data can repair 24 blocks, but 37 blocks are damaged or missing.
gui.recovery.verify_before_failed_math: Run a real recovery verify job before showing failed block math.
gui.recovery.detected_two_groups: Detected across two data groups and one missing volume.
gui.recovery.par2_capacity_24: Existing PAR2 sidecar can repair up to 24 blocks.
gui.recovery.verify_par2_capacity_first: Verify with PAR2 before reporting capacity.
gui.recovery.list_readable_no_full_claim: List readable entries and do not claim full repair.
gui.recovery.no_destructive_write_from_s: No destructive write can start from this state.
gui.recovery.protect_repair_body: PAR2 protects standard archives. ZIP index rebuild handles missing central directories when payloads are intact.
gui.recovery.requires_existing_data: Repair requires PAR2 or SQZ data
gui.recovery.tolerate_loss_body: Shown as real repair capacity first; percentage is secondary to avoid false expectations.
gui.recovery.damage_within_rs_capacity: Detected damage is within the available Reed-Solomon recovery capacity.
gui.recovery.sqz_embedded_body: SQZ embedded recovery can rewrite repaired bytes into a new archive when damage is within capacity.
gui.recovery.par2_sidecars_body: PAR2 sidecars are available for standard archives after protection data exists.
gui.recovery.open_before_capabilities: Open an archive before checking recovery capabilities.
gui.recovery.sidecar_storage_body: Standard 7Z remains untouched. Sidecar files are stored next to the archive.
gui.create.classic_intro: Edit rules first; source preflight runs before the task starts.
gui.create.profile.balanced.detail: Good ratio without making the Mac feel busy
gui.create.profile.custom.detail: User-defined level saved for future jobs
gui.create.format.zip.password: Data encryption only; file names remain visible
gui.create.format.zip.split: ZIP split is supported through .zip/.zNN sets
gui.create.format.zip.recovery: Use PAR2 after create for open-format recovery
gui.create.format.zip.note: Best for sharing with Windows and built-in tools

gui.create.format.7z.note: Best ratio and privacy for long-term archives
gui.create.format.sqz.password: No launch encryption claim; use standard encrypted inner archives when needed
gui.create.format.sqz.split: SQZV split volumes with recovery sidecars
gui.create.format.sqz.recovery: Embedded recovery is available through SQZ workflows
gui.create.format.sqz.note: Best when self-repair and export are more important than ubiquity
gui.create.format.tar.zst.note: Fast developer archives with strong compression speed
gui.create.format.wim.password: No Squallz password layer for WIM
gui.create.format.wim.split: Split WIM is not a launch claim
gui.create.format.wim.recovery: Use PAR2 sidecar after image creation
gui.create.format.wim.note: Requires SQUALLZ_WIMLIB or wimlib-imagex on PATH
